title: WOS Correspondence Metadata Config
description: A WOS Correspondence Metadata Config sidecar document. Declares the metadata schema for correspondence entries stored in case state. Government workflows track correspondence (letters, phone calls, emails, portal submissions, in-person interactions) as part of the case record. The kernel's existing event mechanism handles correspondence events -- events that match no transition are recorded in provenance (Kernel S4.9). This sidecar defines structured metadata for consistent cataloging and retrieval without adding new event types or modifying lifecycle semantics.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)

$wosCorrespondenceMetadata.description: WOS Correspondence Metadata Config specification version marker. MUST be exactly '1.0'. Processors use this field to detect the document type and select the appropriate schema version for validation. Omitting or misspelling this marker causes the document to be rejected as an unrecognized document type.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker for WOS Correspondence Metadata Config sidecar

targetWorkflow.description: Absolute URI of the WOS Kernel Document this correspondence configuration applies to. Processors use this field to bind the sidecar to its parent workflow and validate that correspondence entries are recorded against the correct case type. MUST be an absolute URI matching the '$id' of the target kernel document.

x-lm.intent: Binds this sidecar to its parent kernel workflow

version.description: Semver version string for this Correspondence Metadata Config document, used for change tracking and compatibility checks.

title.description: Human-readable display name for this correspondence configuration document, shown in tooling and audit reports.

description.description: Human-readable summary explaining what kinds of correspondence this configuration covers and which workflow it supports.
examples: Defines metadata templates for all inbound and outbound correspondence in the benefits adjudication workflow.

correspondenceField.description: Dot-separated case state field path where correspondence entries are stored as an array. The workflow processor appends correspondence entry objects to this path when correspondence events are recorded in provenance. MUST refer to an array-typed field in the case state.

x-lm.intent: Identifies which case state field holds the array of correspondence entries

entryTemplates.description: Templates defining the metadata structure for correspondence entries. Each template declares the channel, direction, actor type, and required fields for a category of correspondence.

examples.description: Physical mail received from applicant or representative

x-lm.intent: Templates that define what metadata each category of correspondence entry must carry

id.description: Unique template identifier. Referenced by correspondence entries via templateRef.

description.description: Human-readable description of the correspondence category this template covers, used in tooling and audit summaries.
examples: Physical mail received from applicant or representative
Phone call with applicant or representative

channel.description: Communication channel. 'in-person': face-to-face contact. 'phone': telephone call. 'mail': physical mail. 'email': electronic mail. 'portal': web portal submission or message. 'fax': facsimile transmission. Extensible via x- prefixed values in the extensions property.

x-lm.intent: Declares the communication channel for this correspondence category

direction.description: Direction of the correspondence. 'inbound': received by the agency. 'outbound': sent by the agency.

actorType.description: Who sent or received the correspondence. 'applicant': the primary case subject. 'representative': authorized representative acting on behalf of the applicant. 'third-party': external party (e.g., employer, medical provider). 'system': automated system-generated correspondence. 'agency': agency staff.

x-lm.intent: Identifies the actor role for this correspondence category

items.description: Name of a correspondence entry field that MUST be present for entries using this template, supplementing the base required fields (templateRef, channel, direction, actorType, contentRef, summary, timestamp).

requiredFields.description: Fields that MUST be present in a correspondence entry using this template, beyond the base metadata (templateRef, channel, direction, actorType, contentRef, summary, timestamp).

templateRef.description: The 'id' of the EntryTemplate this correspondence entry conforms to. Processors validate that the referenced template exists in the sidecar's 'entryTemplates' array and that all template 'requiredFields' are present in this entry.

channel.description: Communication channel used for this specific correspondence entry. MUST match the 'channel' declared in the referenced EntryTemplate. Allowed values: 'in-person', 'phone', 'mail', 'email', 'portal', 'fax'.

direction.description: Indicates whether this correspondence was received by the agency ('inbound') or sent by the agency ('outbound'). MUST match the 'direction' in the referenced EntryTemplate.

actorType.description: Role of the actor who sent or received this correspondence. MUST match the 'actorType' in the referenced EntryTemplate. Allowed values: 'applicant', 'representative', 'third-party', 'system', 'agency'.

contentRef.description: Claim check reference -- a URI or storage reference for the actual content. The content itself is not stored in case state; this is a pointer to it.

x-lm.intent: Pointer to the actual correspondence content stored outside case state

summary.description: Human-readable one-line summary describing the substance of this correspondence event, used in audit logs and case timelines. Should be concise and actor-neutral.
examples: Adverse decision notice mailed to applicant

relatedTaskRef.description: Optional reference to a task this correspondence relates to.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 date-time recording when the correspondence event occurred, used for ordering case history and SLA calculations. MUST be in UTC or include a timezone offset.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosKernel': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.
